sections.about: This metric measures discharge to groundwater under BRSR Principle 6 Essential Indicator water discharge by destination. Discharge to groundwater is environmentally sensitive because of aquifer contamination and regulatory risk. A lower value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: By FY2025, all four peers report zero discharge to groundwater. Tata improves from 3,220 kL in FY2024 to zero in FY2025. SAIL reports 22.36 million kL in FY2023 and zero thereafter, suggesting a major treatment/discharge-routing or reporting reclassification. Jindal’s FY2022-23 was not reported and then reported zero in FY2024/FY2025.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2025, Tata is tied 1st with all peers at zero. The gap is zero. Tata’s ranking moves from 4th in FY2024 to tied 1st in FY2025, reflecting elimination of reported groundwater discharge.
sections.targets: Tata’s ZED objective and internal reuse of treated effluents directly support reduction of discharge to groundwater. SAIL’s ZLD goal also supports this metric.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Jindal FY2022-23 was not reported. SAIL’s FY2022-23 to FY2023-24 movement may include reclassification.
